sources.issuing_organisation: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee for Diabetes
sources.exact_document_title: 9. Pharmacologic Approaches to Glycemic Treatment: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation
sources.population: People with diabetes receiving clinician-led pharmacological treatment, with diabetes-type, age, pregnancy, comorbidity and treatment-specific qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Diabetes medication selection, review, intensification, insulin initiation and treatment-plan follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting person-centred medication review, glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, preferences and clinician-decided insulin-initiation context. No medicine, dose or change is mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.2–9.19 and Choice of Glucose-Lowering Therapy — person-centred treatment review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.20–9.30 and insulin-treatment discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented severe hyperglycaemia context, insulin discussion, monitoring, education, treatment burden and follow-up when insulin is being considered or used. All product, route and dose content is excluded.
